title: Terpenoids. Part XCVII : Synthesis of Methylether of Elvirol
author: O. P. VIG
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Department of Chemistry, Panjab University, Chandigarh 

  Manuscript received  28  September  1974;  revised  18  February  1975;  accepted  18  Mardi  1975 

 2-Methoxy-5-methylacetophenone(III) on condensation with ethyl cyano-acetate gives acrylate (IV). The compound (IV) on hydrogenation with Pd/C followed by hydrolysis affords acid (VI). Esterification of (VI) and subsequent reduction provides carbinol (VIII) which on reaction with PBr 3  gives bromide (IX). Treatment of (IX) with NaCN furnishes nitrile (X) which on alcoholysis with HCl-EtOH affords ester (XI). Reduction of (XI) and subsequent oxidation with Collins' reagent provides aldehyde (XIII). Wittig reaction wtith isopropylidenetriphenylphosphorane on aldehyde (XIII) yields the methyl ether of elvirol (II).
steps_raw:
